The extension client project (ENG-2711..2730) is the TS consumer, not the contracts repo. No fix-spec (ENG-2696..2710) touches it. Nothing owns the landing.

Genuinely unticketed: feature-map.txt has zero hits for delivery/extension-delivery/mintDeliveryToken/canary; breakdown/contracts/plan.json tops out at E1-S6/E2-S3 (identity.yaml issuer-registry only, no mintDeliveryToken) with no B8 and no extension/ schema dir; fix-specs.json (ENG-2696..2710) has no contracts-repo entry; ENG-2690 is confirmed (via 20+ extension story bodies) to be Definition-Pack-authoring-only, never a build/landing ticket. Not duplicated by any sibling ext-sweep file (architecture.json/memory-loop.json/prior-art.json/addendum.json/verdict.json all grep-clean for extension-delivery.yaml/mintDeliveryToken). proposed_issue.title: [contracts] Land the extension Delivery-Seam contract — extension-delivery.yaml + identity mintDeliveryToken + 5 shared schemas + 9 golden fixtures + 9 additive errorCodes + SEAMS.md
proposed_issue.milestone: NEW: B8 — Extension delivery seam contract landing
proposed_issue.body_md:
## 🎯 Story

As a **build agent implementing the cross-AI delivery seam in isolation**, I want the ratified Delivery-Seam contract artifacts LANDED verbatim in `orvex-studio-contracts` (authored shape → committed, validated, and pinnable files), so that `orvex-studio-api`, `orvex-studio-identity`, and the extension TS-client consumer all compile and drift-check against ONE pinned agreement instead of re-deciding shapes.

**Definition of Done:** the contracts repo's own self-validation CI (`validate.yaml`, ENG-2091) passes with all new artifacts present AND the golden-fixture byte round-trip is green — each `fixtures/extension/*.request.json` validates against its request schema and each `*.response.json` against its response schema, per the section-8 byte-exact claim. This story is DISPATCH-BLOCKED until the Wave-1 contracts seam lands (ENG-2037 + ENG-2091: tag naming scheme, `validate.yaml`, AGPL guard) — the spec authors shape only and asserts no tag exists yet. [Source: mcRYD29C6o §11]

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — `openapi/extension-delivery.yaml` (server `orvex-studio-api`, `x-seam: extension-delivery`) declares exactly `validateCompose` (POST /v1/deliveries/validate), `reportDeliveryOutcome` (POST /v1/deliveries/outcome), `ingestCanaryTelemetry` (POST /v1/telemetry/canary) — all BEARER-tier — with the section-5 component bodies. *assert: 3 operations, correct verbs/paths, bodies present.* [Source: mcRYD29C6o §2, §5]
- [ ] **AC2** — `mintDeliveryToken` (POST /v1/delivery-tokens, 201) is added to existing `openapi/identity.yaml` (server `orvex-studio-identity`) with `DeliveryTokenMintRequest` `{memoryRef, target, composedTextRef, assertedScope}`. *assert: op present in identity.yaml, request schema matches §5.1.* [Source: mcRYD29C6o §2.3, §5.1]
- [ ] **AC3** — The FIVE `extension/*.schema.json` shared schemas exist (`delivery-token`, `delivery-target`, `composed-text-ref`, `asserted-scope`, `failure-class`), JSON-Schema 2020-12, `additionalProperties:false`, `$id` on the real `https://github.com/orvexai/orvex-studio-contracts/...` base (never dereferenced), same-dir `$ref` by bare filename and cross-dir by `../identity/...`. *assert: 5 files, $id/$ref hygiene per §4.* [Source: mcRYD29C6o §4]
- [ ] **AC4** — `DeliveryTokenScope.user` `$ref`s `../identity/principal.schema.json` (NOT `identity/token-scope.schema.json`, which lacks subject/tenant) and `DeliveryTarget` is the single promoted `extension/delivery-target.schema.json` `$ref`'d by both mint scope and validate request. *assert: no duplicated DeliveryTarget; user references principal.* [Source: mcRYD29C6o §4.1, §4.5, §10]
- [ ] **AC5** — The NINE `fixtures/extension/` request+response pairs land byte-exact: real 64-hex `composedTextRef.hash` (no ellipsis), no `//` comment lines in JSON, `scope.user` a valid `principal` object, mint-scope-mismatch negative using the real `_components/common.yaml` `Error` `{errorCode, message, details?}` envelope. All validate green. *assert: 18 files, round-trip green in validate CI.* [Source: mcRYD29C6o §8]
- [ ] **AC6** — The NINE additive errorCodes (`REQUEST_MALFORMED`, `CONSENT_SCOPE_MISMATCH`, `DELIVERY_FIREWALL_REFUSED`, `DELIVERY_TOKEN_INVALID`, `DELIVERY_TOKEN_EXPIRED`, `DELIVERY_TOKEN_SPENT`, `CONTENT_HASH_MISMATCH`, `DELIVERY_TARGET_MISMATCH`, `CONSENT_REVOKED`) are APPENDED to `errors/vocabulary.yaml` (never reordered) and `errors/vocabulary.order.txt` is regenerated; NOTHING added to `errors/exit-codes.yaml`. *assert: 9 codes appended, order.txt regenerated, exit-codes untouched.* [Source: mcRYD29C6o §7, §12]
- [ ] **AC7** — `SEAMS.md` NFR-C4 inventory gains the four operations; NOTHING is added to `events/catalog.yaml` (Shape 3 is not a CloudEvent) and no `composeInto` / Shape 1b symbol is added anywhere. *assert: SEAMS.md has 4 ops; catalog + composeInto untouched.* [Source: mcRYD29C6o §2.4, §6, §9, §12]
- [ ] **AC8** (negative) — A PR that adds a `composeInto` path/schema/fixture, a 5th operation, a standalone top-level `providerId` on `ComposeValidationRequest`, or a `studio.*` canary event is rejected at review. *assert: exclusions enforced.* [Source: mcRYD29C6o §5.2, §6]

## 🔨 Tasks

- [ ] Add `openapi/extension-delivery.yaml` (§2 skeletons + §5 bodies); error `$ref`s point at bare `_components/common.yaml#/...` (NOT `errors.yaml`, NOT `../_components/...`).
- [ ] Add `mintDeliveryToken` to `openapi/identity.yaml` (§2.3 + §5.1).
- [ ] Add the five `extension/*.schema.json` verbatim (§4, incl. promoted `delivery-target.schema.json` §4.5).
- [ ] Add the nine `fixtures/extension/` pairs (§8), one JSON per named heading, byte-exact.
- [ ] Append the nine errorCodes to `errors/vocabulary.yaml`; regenerate `errors/vocabulary.order.txt`.
- [ ] Add the four ops to `SEAMS.md`; leave `events/catalog.yaml` untouched.
- [ ] Run `validate.yaml` + fixture round-trip green; everything lands at `x-status: draft`.

## 🧠 Context

**🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** filed from the ratified extension canon sweep.

Authoritative shape = Contract Spec `mcRYD29C6o` (ENG-2690 Task 3, ratified 2026-07-15), transcribed from architecture `ViWUZj1MrW` §8 and AD-EXT-3/6/8. This is the BUILD (land-the-files) half; `mcRYD29C6o` did the AUTHOR half and `ENG-2091` owns the Wave-1 tag the artifacts pin under — this ticket is neither. Additive-only change (new files, new symbols; no existing `required` set or the FROZEN ENG-1533 `Error` envelope reshaped), so it rides ADR-0008's automated lane (drift-gate + semver + AGPL-import + schema-validation are the only police). The tag itself is authored-for, not asserted (mcRYD29C6o §11).

## 🧪 Testing

Contracts self-validation CI (`validate.yaml`): OpenAPI 3.1 + JSON-Schema 2020-12 lint, cross-ref resolution, `$id`/servers hygiene, and the nine-fixture byte round-trip (each request→request-schema, each response→response-schema). Negative coverage: the compose-hash-mismatch 200-union member and the mint-scope-mismatch 403 `Error` envelope must both round-trip. No engine checkout, no network — reproducible from the tag alone (A-CODEGEN). [Source: mcRYD29C6o §8, §9]

## 📏 Guidance

CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §§3.7 (design-it-twice sketches recorded: refused-as-200-union vs 403, HTTP-ingest vs CloudEvent) / §10 (non-conflation — canary is operational health, not a domain event). SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t`: highest-blast-radius seam surface — additive precision, no reshaping. cell-lint N/A (authored artifact repo).

## 🔗 References

Contract Spec `mcRYD29C6o` (§1 file map, §2 ops, §4 schemas, §7 errorCodes, §8 fixtures, §12 handoff checklist); Architecture `ViWUZj1MrW` §8 + AD-EXT-3/6/8; PRD `g9vWbSYplh` F1.

## 🔗 Dependencies

- [ ] Blocked by: **ENG-2690** (this spec — authored shape, the source of truth).
- [ ] Blocked by: **ENG-2091** (tag naming scheme + `validate.yaml` self-validation CI + AGPL guard the artifacts pin under) and **ENG-2037** (Wave-1 contracts seam landing) — fixtures cannot go green until these exist.
- [ ] Blocks: the api serve-ops story (validate/outcome/canary) and the extension TS-client codegen (ENG-2712) — both pin this tag.

## 📡 Protocol

CLAIM → PLAN → PROGRESS → COMMITS ("Part of ENG-NNN", never closes) → HANDOFF → REVIEW (reviewer ≠ implementer) → TICK → DONE (orchestrator-only) → ESCALATE.

gaps.requirement: IMPLEMENT + SERVE the three api-surface Delivery-Seam operations on orvex-studio-api: validateCompose (validate token/scope/consent + presented.composedTextRef == token.composedTextRef by HASH EQUALITY only, RESERVE the token, do NOT spend, do NOT audit); reportDeliveryOutcome (client reports terminal injected|copied|aborted, api THEN spends the single-use token and writes the ONE authoritative compliance audit reflecting the real delivery mechanism, returns an acknowledged receipt); ingestCanaryTelemetry (HTTP ingest of one classified breakage event, re-emitted as OTLP metric canary_trip_total{provider,class,selectorPackVersion} + a Loki log line, never a CloudEvent).
gaps.quote: Shape 1a (compose-VALIDATION + outcome-report) and Shape 3 (canary telemetry ingest) are `orvex-studio-api` operations (`api` is the sole ingesting Orvex service, §3)... validateCompose ... RESERVES the token; does NOT mark it spent; does NOT write the audit. reportDeliveryOutcome ... `api` THEN spends the single-use token and writes the ONE authoritative compliance audit reflecting the real mechanism. ingestCanaryTelemetry ... `api` re-emits it as an OTLP metric (canary_trip_total{provider,class,selectorPackVersion}) and a Loki log line.
gaps.why_uncovered: The api breakdown (B1-B9 / ENG-2098 pack) predates the extension program. B4 'Prompt Composer & enrichment' E4-S2 is the SERVER composer's own private-memory guard + egress audit (reuses E3-S3 consent + E1-S6 audit) — it never serves the untrusted-browser wire ops, never reserves/spends a delivery token, and never writes the AD-EXT-6 authoritative delivery audit. No api epic hosts validate/outcome/canary. The extension client stories ENG-2711..2730 are client-side (adapters, local consent record, canary detection) and ENG-2448 AC5 explicitly punts the compose surface to api/ui. Identity's ENG-2448 owns only the mint. The api ingest/reservation/spend/audit/OTLP-re-emit half is owned by no ticket.
gaps.verdict_reason: The underlying requirement (api must serve the 3 delivery-seam ops) is real and genuinely absent from the 575-story corpus — but this specific gap ENTRY is a triplicate within the same ext-sweep run, not a standalone find: ext-sweep/architecture.json proposes '[api] Shape 1a compose-validation + outcome-report' AND '[api] Shape 3 canary-telemetry ingest', both under the identical new milestone 'B10 — Cross-AI delivery seam (extension server counterparties)'; ext-sweep/memory-loop.json independently proposes the same two split tickets under 'NEW: B — Cross-AI delivery server half'. Both siblings are more complete than this entry: they correctly include ENG-2037 (Wave-1 contracts gate) and ENG-2690 (extension Definition Pack — source of the op shapes) in blocked_by, whereas THIS gap's proposed_issue blocked_by ([ENG-2098, ENG-2091, ENG-2448]) omits both — a real defect, not just style. Filing this entry as written would create a 3rd/4th overlapping 'B10 api delivery seam' ticket for identical work already twice-proposed with finer-grained (Shape 1a vs Shape 3), more accurately-blocked tickets elsewhere in this same gap-hunt output. Recommend: drop this entry from ext-sweep dispatch and let architecture.json/memory-loop.json's pair of tickets (after their own dedup) cover the api half; do not fix-in-place because the correct fix is 'do not file a 3rd copy', not a text edit to this proposed_issue.

proposed_issue.title: [api] Serve the extension Delivery-Seam api ops — validateCompose (reserve + hash-equality), reportDeliveryOutcome (spend + the one authoritative audit), ingestCanaryTelemetry (OTLP + Loki re-emit)
proposed_issue.milestone: NEW: B10 — Extension delivery seam (api ingest surface)
proposed_issue.body_md:
## 🎯 Story

As a **privacy-conscious user delivering a Memory-enriched prompt through the browser extension**, I want `orvex-studio-api` to be the sole ingesting service that VALIDATES my compose against the identity-minted delivery token, RESERVES then SPENDS it single-use, writes the ONE authoritative compliance audit of what actually happened, and ingests canary breakage telemetry — so the untrusted client can never compose without a fresh server verdict and every real delivery leaves exactly one audit trail (AD-EXT-6).

**Definition of Done:** one named integration test **`TestDeliverySeamReserveSpendAudit`** — drives `validateCompose` (returns `validated` + `reservationId` with `tokenStatus:reserved`, token NOT spent, no audit written), then `reportDeliveryOutcome` (api spends the single-use token, writes exactly ONE authoritative audit with the real `outcome`, returns `acknowledged`+`auditId`+`tokenStatus:spent`), and asserts a differing re-report on a spent token is 409 `DELIVERY_TOKEN_SPENT` while a same-outcome re-report is idempotent 200. Dispatch-BLOCKED until the delivery-seam contract tag lands (the contracts landing story + ENG-2091) — the ops are pinned against `openapi/extension-delivery.yaml`. [Source: mcRYD29C6o §2.1, §2.2, §5.2, §5.3]

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — Given a presented `deliveryToken` + `target` + `composedTextRef`, When `POST /v1/deliveries/validate`, Then api validates token/scope/consent AND `presented.composedTextRef == token.composedTextRef` by HASH EQUALITY only (never re-derives text), RESERVES the token, returns `{decision:validated, reservationId, tokenStatus:reserved, expiresAt}` — token NOT spent, no audit written. *assert: reserved, not spent, no audit row.* [Source: mcRYD29C6o §2.1, §5.2]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given a differing presented `composedTextRef` on an active reservation, When validated, Then `{decision:refused, refusalClass:CONTENT_HASH_MISMATCH}` (200 union) and the existing reservation is untouched; a target/token/consent mismatch returns the matching `RefusalClass` (`DELIVERY_TARGET_MISMATCH` / `DELIVERY_TOKEN_INVALID|EXPIRED|SPENT` / `CONSENT_REVOKED`) as a 200 refused verdict, NOT a transport error. *assert: refused verdicts are 200-union, reservation stable.* [Source: mcRYD29C6o §5.2, §7]
- [ ] **AC3** — Given `validateCompose` is per-token idempotent while RESERVED, When called again with the SAME token+hash, Then it returns the ORIGINAL `reservationId`/`expiresAt` (never a second reservation). *assert: same reservationId on repeat.* [Source: mcRYD29C6o §5.2]
- [ ] **AC4** — Given `POST /v1/deliveries/outcome` with terminal `outcome` (injected|copied|aborted) + optional `failureClass`, When received, Then api SPENDS the single-use token and writes exactly ONE authoritative compliance audit reflecting the real mechanism, returns `{acknowledged:true, auditId, tokenStatus:spent}`; a GO-canary-fail-to-copy reports the SAME token's outcome as `copied` with no second mint. *assert: single spend, one audit, no re-mint.* [Source: mcRYD29C6o §2.2, §5.3]
- [ ] **AC5** (error-path) — Given a spent token, When a DIFFERENT outcome is re-reported, Then `409 DELIVERY_TOKEN_SPENT`; a SAME-outcome re-report returns the original ack idempotently (200); an unknown token/reservation is `404 NOT_FOUND`. *assert: 409 on conflict, idempotent 200, 404 on unknown.* [Source: mcRYD29C6o §5.3]
- [ ] **AC6** — Given `POST /v1/telemetry/canary` with a classified breakage event `{providerId, selectorPackVersion, failureClass, tier, timestamp}`, When ingested, Then api returns `202 acknowledged` and re-emits an OTLP metric `canary_trip_total{provider,class,selectorPackVersion}` + a Loki log line; it is NEVER modeled as a CloudEvent on studio-spine and NOT added to `events/catalog.yaml`. *assert: 202, OTLP+Loki emitted, zero broker publish.* [Source: mcRYD29C6o §2.4, §5.4]
- [ ] **AC7** — All four surfaces consume the codegen'd types from the PINNED contracts tag (no hand-rolled shapes); malformed body → `400 REQUEST_MALFORMED`, absent/expired session → `401 UNAUTHORIZED`, over-quota → `402 QUOTA_EXCEEDED`, throttle → `429 RATE_LIMITED`, all on the shared `Error` envelope. *assert: served OpenAPI diff-clean against the tag; error codes match §7.* [Source: mcRYD29C6o §7, §9]

## 🔨 Tasks

- [ ] RED: `TestDeliverySeamReserveSpendAudit` (reserve→spend→audit; 409/idempotent/404 branches; hash-mismatch refused-union).
- [ ] GREEN: `validateCompose` — token/scope/consent validation + hash-equality check + reservation store (no spend, no audit).
- [ ] GREEN: `reportDeliveryOutcome` — single-use spend + the ONE authoritative compliance audit (real mechanism) + `acknowledged` receipt; same-token copy-fallback path.
- [ ] GREEN: `ingestCanaryTelemetry` — 202 ingest → OTLP `canary_trip_total{provider,class,selectorPackVersion}` metric + Loki log; no CloudEvent.
- [ ] Consume codegen'd client from the pinned delivery-seam tag; wire the shared `Error` envelope + 400/401/402/429 responses.

## 🧠 Context

**🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** filed from the ratified extension canon sweep.

AD-EXT-3 ordering invariant: `validateCompose` returns `validated` strictly BEFORE the client canary (Shape 1b) runs, and reservation is per-token idempotent while reserved. AD-EXT-6: consent binds at the mint boundary (identity, ENG-2448) — api is the ingesting/enforcing side that reserves, spends, and writes the sole authoritative audit reflecting the REAL delivery mechanism (`injected` vs copy/paste fallback). The api-composer's own private-memory egress audit (E4-S2) is a DIFFERENT artifact and does NOT cover these untrusted-browser wire ops. Shape 3 is operational health, not a memory-domain state change — HTTP ingest + OTLP/Loki re-emission only (CS §10 non-conflation). [Source: mcRYD29C6o §2, §3]

## 🧪 Testing

Named DoD test `TestDeliverySeamReserveSpendAudit` (integration). Tiers: unit (hash-equality compare, refusal-class mapping, idempotency state machine) + integration (reserve→spend→audit round-trip, canary→OTLP/Loki). CS §5: exercise the token reservation/spend + audit real; the identity mint (ENG-2448) and the client compose are cross-service boundaries stubbed at their edges. Negative: hash-mismatch refused-union, spent-token 409, unknown-token 404. [Source: mcRYD29C6o §5.2, §5.3]

## 📏 Guidance

CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §§4/5/10/11 (audit chokepoint, consent enforcement, non-conflation, security). SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t`: Security (single-use spend, hash-equality-only never re-derive, fail-closed refusals) + Observability (canary OTLP/Loki, no broker egress). cell-lint `JGAUQRsw2g` (audit events cell-stamped).

## 🔗 References

Contract Spec `mcRYD29C6o` (§2 ops, §5.2/5.3/5.4 bodies, §7 errorCodes); Architecture `ViWUZj1MrW` §8 + AD-EXT-3/6/8; PRD `g9vWbSYplh` F1 (FR-D4/D5/D7). Identity mint counterpart: ENG-2448 (AD-8).

## 🔗 Dependencies

- [ ] Blocked by: **ENG-2098** (api Definition Pack — contract TAG is the dispatch gate) and the delivery-seam contract landing (extension-delivery.yaml pinned) + **ENG-2091** (the tag) — the ops codegen against the pinned tag.
- [ ] Blocked by: **ENG-2448** (identity `mintDeliveryToken` — tokens must exist to validate/spend).
- [ ] Cross-service: extension client (ENG-2711..2730, presents tokens + reports outcomes); orvex-studio-console/LGTM (consumes the canary OTLP metric + Loki log).

## 📡 Protocol

CLAIM → PLAN → PROGRESS → COMMITS ("Part of ENG-NNN", never closes) → HANDOFF → REVIEW (reviewer ≠ implementer) → TICK → DONE (orchestrator-only) → ESCALATE.
